title: Was Asuka fetish fuel for anyone else
display_title: Was Asuka fetish fuel for anyone else
wikitext:
[]: Was Asuka fetish fuel for anyone else?             Quote Post by SeanTucker » Thu Nov 06, 2008 9:00 pm I now have a thing for tsunderes solely because of Asuka. Hell, my current crush is basically a shorter Asuka with black hair and slightly darker skin. I get the feeling I'm not the only one who Eva had such an... ahem, profound effect on. So, I'm asking EGF.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Nov 06, 2008 9:04 pm My first girlfriend was an Asuka. Didn't work out. My current girlfriend is more of a Rei, but can be a real Misato at times. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Nov 06, 2008 10:20 pm In before "Asuka isn't really a tsundere". Asuka's status as fetish fuel is an old and sad state of affairs that we don't need a confessionals thread to demonstrate.

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Thu Nov 06, 2008 10:26 pm Sachi_13 wrote: My first girlfriend was an Asuka. Didn't work out. My current girlfriend is more of a Rei, but can be a real Misato at times. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Nov 06, 2008 11:11 pm Themaninblack wrote: Verdit: Cool for a friend with benfits, but horrible for a girlfriend.

[]:             Quote Post by SeanTucker » Thu Nov 06, 2008 11:14 pm Reichu wrote: In before "Asuka isn't really a tsundere".

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Thu Nov 06, 2008 11:17 pm Your girlfriends are "side dishes" for Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Thu Nov 06, 2008 11:47 pm Asuka isn't really a tsundere. That aside, tsundere is the love/hate thing directed mostly at the love interest. While Shinji being Asuka's (sort-of) love interest, she really only directs the hatred side to Shinji and never shows him the crush.

[]:             Quote Post by pat457 » Fri Nov 07, 2008 9:35 am Sachi_13 wrote: My first girlfriend was an Asuka. Didn't work out. My current girlfriend is more of a Rei, but can be a real Misato at times. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Chowwow » Sat Nov 08, 2008 12:33 am Asuka's status as fetish fuel is an old and sad state of affairs that we don't need a confessionals thread to demonstrate.

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel² » Sat Nov 08, 2008 5:08 pm Chowwow wrote: just like why we have SO MANY GODDAMN Rei clones in nearly every anime these days.

[]:             Quote Post by wintermute » Sat Nov 08, 2008 10:07 pm Ramiel² wrote: They are really annoying, Girls at schools which have an apearrance like they woked out of a coma and forgotten how to speak.. Can't see them anymore..>_>

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sat Nov 08, 2008 10:54 pm i know some Asuka's in my school, but they're more asshole than good-looking, though. and i'm not talking about my ex girlfriends. they're a little different. I promised myself that I would not compare my girlfriends to anyone or anything, and that they would exist as independent experiences, not as something to be compared to. /offtopic i don't know what a tsundere is, but Asuka is warmingly arrogant during the "munch munch sandwich scene", enough to make Shinji smile, and Asuka doesn't stop this. perhaps she is getting blinded by looking at the sun, or perhaps she doesn't mind, or doesn't care. idano. excluding that scene, i don't think she fits what a tsundere is. firstly, she pilots Eva to show the world how cool she is. that just screams egomaniac, and not somebody with a "spiky personality but actually modest and loving". One can call what Asuka and Shinji did at the conclusion of EoE as "modest" or "loving", though...depending on the interpretation.

[]:             Quote Post by mrpanda » Sun Nov 09, 2008 6:35 pm Ive met a few hot 'Asuka' girls although their not worth the effort tbh, high strung girls like that are good for short passionate relationships at best. Asuka is tsundere in the way that she treats Shinji like shit but deep down needs him to balence her own emotions out.

[]:             Quote Post by Soljer13 » Mon Nov 10, 2008 10:58 am My Wife is an Asuka in looks, but she's totaly a Misato.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Mon Nov 10, 2008 9:45 pm isn't Misato an older version of Asuka? IIRC that was the common consensus.../offtopic

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Nov 10, 2008 9:50 pm Sounds like a misconception.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Nov 11, 2008 3:45 am There is quite a bit they have in common... and I don't mean just the way they comb their bangs, color preferences, or taste in men. Both have intimacy issues because of childhood abandonment issues and trauma. Both are highly intelligent, driven individuals who have no compunction breaking rules or hurting others to get things done they see as needed. Both are fond of verbal teasing though neither can take a come-back very well due to relatively fragile egos. Neither has any domestic skills to speak of but are willing and able to use their looks to make others handle such details. Both have outgoing, flirtatious personalities and prefer friends who are intelligent, authoritative, but not really competition for the spotlight... and with whom they are reluctant to share anything personal. Both disapprove of the romantic choices of those friends and vehemently deny their own attractions when asked by said friends. Both are likely to resort to threats of (or actual) violence to resolve problems and are quite capable of backing up their mouths. Both are willing to volunteer for suicidally ridiculous risks to life and limb if it will accomplish their immediate goals. That enough for you?

[]: Rei vs Asuka             Quote Post by bioweapon680 » Tue Nov 11, 2008 10:29 pm If I ever met Asuka and had to be stuck with her, chances are I might slap her because I can't really stand her insensitive personality. Rei, on the other hand, would lead to very very awkward silences. However, I still believe Rei is a better candidate. And what did AyanamiOtaku mean by the "Munch munch sandwich scene"?

[]: Re: Rei vs Asuka             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Nov 11, 2008 10:42 pm bioweapon680 wrote: And what did AyanamiOtaku mean by the "Munch munch sandwich scene"?
